texto: I don't usually like teen novels written in the present tense, particularly those told from a first-person viewpoint. Too many writers seem to believe that using either or both devices automatically imbues their stories with deep seriousness and a contemporary feel.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tony Bradman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't usually like teen novels written in the present tense, particularly those told from a fir…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, deep, believe pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de believe e present.

frase_original: I don't usually like teen novels written in the present tense, particularly those told from a first-person viewpoint. Too many writers seem to believe that using either or both devices automatically imbues their stories with deep seriousness and a contemporary feel.

texto: I have to confess that I've never been a great fan of Christmas or, as it's known in our house, The Monster That Ate the Last Third of the Year. It's mostly the rampant consumerism I object to, but I'm also a little wary of the annual crop of new Christmas stories and sometimes wonder why anyone bothers.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Tony Bradman sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “I have to confess that I've never been a great fan of Christmas or, as it's known in our house, T…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a tempo, great, why, útil em momentos de natal e why.

frase_original: I have to confess that I've never been a great fan of Christmas or, as it's known in our house, The Monster That Ate the Last Third of the Year. It's mostly the rampant consumerism I object to, but I'm also a little wary of the annual crop of new Christmas stories and sometimes wonder why anyone bothers.
